title: Installation and Setup
description: Learn how to install Playwright, set up your development environment, and configure your first testing project.
objectives: Install Playwright in a Node.js project
Understand the project structure and configuration files
Set up your IDE for optimal Playwright development

sections.title: Prerequisites
sections.content:
# Prerequisites

Before installing Playwright, ensure you have:

## Node.js

Playwright requires Node.js version 16 or higher. Check your version:

```bash
node --version
```

If you need to install or update Node.js, download it from [nodejs.org](https://nodejs.org/).

## Package Manager

You'll need either npm (comes with Node.js), yarn, or pnpm. We'll use npm in our examples, but any package manager works.

## Text Editor or IDE

We recommend:
- **Visual Studio Code** (with Playwright Test extension)
- WebStorm
- Any editor with TypeScript support

## System Requirements

- **Windows**: Windows 10 or higher
- **macOS**: macOS 11 or higher
- **Linux**: Debian-based (Ubuntu, etc.) or Fedora-based distributions
- At least 2GB of free disk space for browser binaries

sections.title: Installation Methods
sections.content:
# Installing Playwright

There are several ways to install Playwright, depending on your needs.

## Method 1: Create a New Project (Recommended)

The easiest way to get started is using Playwright's init command:

```bash
npm init playwright@latest
```

This interactive command will:
1. Install Playwright and its dependencies
2. Create a basic project structure
3. Add example tests
4. Set up configuration files
5. Install browser binaries

During initialization, you'll be asked:
- Where to put your tests (default: `tests` or `e2e`)
- Whether to add a GitHub Actions workflow
- Whether to install Playwright browsers

**Pro Tip**: Answer 'yes' to installing browsers - this downloads Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit.

## Method 2: Add to Existing Project

If you already have a Node.js project:

```bash
npm install --save-dev @playwright/test
npx playwright install
```

The first command installs the Playwright test runner, and the second downloads browser binaries.

## Method 3: Install Specific Components

For more control:

```bash
# Install test runner
npm install --save-dev @playwright/test

# Install only specific browsers
npx playwright install chromium
npx playwright install firefox
npx playwright install webkit

# Install with dependencies (on Linux)
npx playwright install --with-deps
```

## Verify Installation

Check that Playwright is installed correctly:

```bash
npx playwright --version
```

You should see the Playwright version number.

sections.title: Installation Example
sections.code:
# Step-by-step installation process

# 1. Create a new directory for your project
mkdir my-playwright-project
cd my-playwright-project

# 2. Initialize a new Node.js project
npm init -y

# 3. Install Playwright
npm init playwright@latest

# The installer will prompt you with questions:
# ✔ Do you want to use TypeScript or JavaScript? · TypeScript
# ✔ Where to put your end-to-end tests? · tests
# ✔ Add a GitHub Actions workflow? · true
# ✔ Install Playwright browsers? · true

# 4. Verify installation
npx playwright --version

# Output: Version 1.40.0 (or your installed version)
sections.explanation: This example shows the complete installation process from scratch. The init command creates a fully configured project ready for testing.

sections.title: Project Structure
sections.content:
# Understanding the Project Structure

After installation, your project will have this structure:

```
my-playwright-project/
├── node_modules/          # Dependencies
├── tests/                 # Your test files
│   └── example.spec.ts   # Example test
├── tests-examples/        # More examples (optional)
│   └── demo-todo-app.spec.ts
├── playwright.config.ts   # Main configuration file
├── package.json          # Node.js project file
└── package-lock.json     # Dependency lock file
```

## Key Files

### playwright.config.ts

This is the main configuration file where you define:
- Test directory locations
- Browser settings
- Timeout values
- Base URLs
- Reporters
- Parallel execution settings

### tests/ directory

Your test files go here. Test files should:
- End with `.spec.ts` (TypeScript) or `.spec.js` (JavaScript)
- Follow a logical organization (e.g., by feature or page)

### package.json

Contains your project dependencies and scripts:

```json
{
  "scripts": {
    "test": "playwright test",
    "test:headed": "playwright test --headed",
    "test:ui": "playwright test --ui",
    "test:debug": "playwright test --debug"
  },
  "devDependencies": {
    "@playwright/test": "^1.40.0"
  }
}
```

sections.title: Configuration File
sections.explanation: This is a typical Playwright configuration file. Each setting is explained with comments. You can customize these values based on your project needs.

sections.title: IDE Setup - VS Code
sections.content:
# Setting Up Visual Studio Code

VS Code is the most popular editor for Playwright development. Here's how to set it up optimally:

## Install the Playwright Extension

1. Open VS Code
2. Go to Extensions (Ctrl+Shift+X or Cmd+Shift+X)
3. Search for "Playwright Test for VSCode"
4. Install the official extension by Microsoft

## Extension Features

The extension provides:

**Test Explorer**: View and run tests from the sidebar
- See all your tests in a tree view
- Run individual tests or test files
- Debug tests with breakpoints

**Code Actions**: Quick actions in the editor
- Run test at cursor
- Debug test at cursor
- Record new test

**IntelliSense**: Auto-completion and type hints
- Method signatures
- Available locators
- Configuration options

## Recommended Settings

Add these to your `.vscode/settings.json`:

```json
{
  "playwright.reuseBrowser": true,
  "playwright.showTrace": true,
  "editor.formatOnSave": true,
  "editor.defaultFormatter": "esbenp.prettier-vscode"
}
```

## Useful Shortcuts

- **Run Test at Cursor**: Click the green triangle next to test
- **Debug Test**: Click the debug icon next to test
- **Record Test**: Click the record button in Test Explorer
- **Show Test Output**: View test results in the Output panel

## Additional Extensions

Consider installing:
- **ESLint**: For code linting
- **Prettier**: For code formatting
- **Error Lens**: Inline error highlighting
- **GitLens**: Enhanced Git integration

sections.title: First Test Run
sections.code:
# Running your first tests

# 1. Run all tests
npx playwright test

# 2. Run tests in headed mode (see the browser)
npx playwright test --headed

# 3. Run a specific test file
npx playwright test tests/example.spec.ts

# 4. Run tests in a specific browser
npx playwright test --project=chromium

# 5. Run tests in UI mode (interactive)
npx playwright test --ui

# 6. Run tests in debug mode
npx playwright test --debug

# 7. Show test report after running
npx playwright show-report

# Expected output:
# Running 3 tests using 3 workers
#   3 passed (2s)
# 
# To open last HTML report run:
#   npx playwright show-report
sections.explanation: These commands show different ways to run Playwright tests. Start with --ui mode to see what's happening, then move to headless for faster execution.

sections.title: Common Setup Issues
sections.content:
# Troubleshooting Common Issues

## Issue 1: Browser Installation Failed

**Problem**: Browsers didn't install during setup

**Solution**:
```bash
npx playwright install
# Or for specific browser:
npx playwright install chromium
```

## Issue 2: Permission Errors on Linux

**Problem**: Missing system dependencies

**Solution**:
```bash
# Install system dependencies
npx playwright install-deps
# Or manually:
sudo apt-get install libgstreamer-plugins-base1.0-0
```

## Issue 3: Port Already in Use

**Problem**: baseURL port is occupied

**Solution**: Change the port in `playwright.config.ts` or stop the conflicting process

## Issue 4: Tests Timing Out

**Problem**: Tests exceed timeout limits

**Solution**: Increase timeout in config:
```typescript
timeout: 60 * 1000, // 60 seconds
```

## Issue 5: VS Code Extension Not Working

**Problem**: Extension doesn't recognize tests

**Solution**:
1. Reload VS Code (Cmd/Ctrl + Shift + P > "Reload Window")
2. Check workspace settings
3. Verify playwright.config.ts is in project root

## Getting Help

If you encounter issues:
1. Check [Playwright Documentation](https://playwright.dev/docs/intro)
2. Search [GitHub Issues](https://github.com/microsoft/playwright/issues)
3. Ask on [Discord](https://aka.ms/playwright/discord)
4. Post on [Stack Overflow](https://stackoverflow.com/questions/tagged/playwright)

keyTakeaways: Playwright requires Node.js 16+ and can be installed with npm init playwright@latest
The installation includes browser binaries for Chromium, Firefox, and WebKit
playwright.config.ts is the main configuration file for test settings
VS Code with the Playwright extension provides the best development experience
Tests should be placed in the tests/ directory with .spec.ts or .spec.js extension
Use npx playwright test --ui for interactive test running and debugging
The project structure is organized and includes example tests to get started
